godot_hook_investigation.note: Investigation only; external/godot-master is NOT modified. Paths and functions are recorded for later mapping and are not wired up.

io_resources_mapping_scaffold.input: hdr_internal_texture / p_source_texture for the first native Godot level
io_resources_mapping_scaffold.output: p_luminance_buffers->current after the final SWAP(current, reduce[last])
io_resources_mapping_scaffold.rurix_scaffold: single-level src_luminance SRV t0 to dst_luminance UAV u0 mapping; not a runtime pyramid replacement
io_resources_mapping_scaffold.note: tracked resource mapping scaffold; not wired as a real runtime pass

dispatch_shape_mapping_scaffold.godot_compute_shape: 8x8 local shader block; each level dispatches over current source_size and then divides source_size by 8 with clamp to 1

descriptor_mapping_scaffold.binding_kind_field: segment 4i added a per-resource 'binding_kind' string field to the descriptor layout emitted by render_descriptor_layout_json. Values are 'texture2d' (SRV Texture2D), 'rwtexture2d' (UAV RWTexture2D), 'raw_buffer_view' (View/ViewMut), 'sampler', or 'constant_buffer'. The canonical tracked luminance kernel (src/lib_texture.rx) emits 'texture2d' for src_luminance and 'rwtexture2d' for dst_luminance; the historical raw-buffer fixture (src/lib.rx, artifacts/raw_buffer_historical/) emits 'raw_buffer_view' for both.

checks: 64-bit integer shader capability flag must be present in RxGdCaps.flags
exactly 2 texture resources in src_luminance (t0) then dst_luminance (u0) order
28-byte b0 root constant block: source_width/source_height as 64-bit integers, then max_luminance/min_luminance/exposure_adjust as 32-bit floats
source dimensions in the b0 block must be nonzero and match the bound src_luminance resource
dst_luminance must have the level-0 reduce shape max(source / 8, 1) per axis
runtime_binding_preflight.on_success: RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK (gate stays disabled; no D3D12 dispatch is recorded)
runtime_binding_preflight.on_failure: RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK with fallback reason validation_failed or unsupported_device; no estimated GPU/CPU time is accumulated
runtime_binding_preflight.native_handle_semantics: logical Godot RID id, not a D3D12 GPU handle; real handle binding belongs to a future gated dispatch slice
runtime_binding_preflight.kernel_binding_kind: texture2d (slot 0 src_luminance SRV t0); slot 1 dst_luminance UAV u0 is rwtexture2d

gated_dispatch_bringup.opt_in_signal: reserved capability flag RXGD_CAP_LUMINANCE_DISPATCH_BRINGUP (1u << 1) carried in RxGdCaps.flags; no C ABI struct layout change and RXGD_ABI_VERSION stays 1
eligibility_checks: segment 4a runtime binding preflight must pass first
Godot dispatch bring-up opt-in flag RXGD_CAP_LUMINANCE_DISPATCH_BRINGUP present in RxGdCaps.flags
64-bit integer shader capability present in RxGdCaps.flags
native D3D12 device and command queue handles are non-null
both resource native handles are non-null

compiled package is available and its descriptor layout (2 resources, 28-byte b0 root constants, SRV t0, UAV u0, 64-bit integer requirement) and offline compile evidence digests (DXIL, root signature, descriptor layout SHA-256) match the tracked evidence
gated_dispatch_bringup.on_ineligible: RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK with fallback reason manual_disabled (opt-in off), unsupported_device (missing capability or null native handles), or validation_failed (null resource handle or layout/digest mismatch); no estimated GPU/CPU time is accumulated
gated_dispatch_bringup.on_eligible: RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK: the explicit dispatch gate stays closed because no linked runtime dispatch kernel exists; no D3D12 dispatch is recorded by the bridge and no estimated GPU/CPU time is accumulated
gated_dispatch_bringup.real_local_d3d12_dispatch_smoke: segment 4c harness ci/grx009_luminance_d3d12_dispatch_smoke.py records measured status=success evidence (see segment_4c_real_d3d12_dispatch_smoke); this is standalone smoke evidence and does not make the bridge record a dispatch or return OK

segment_4c_real_d3d12_dispatch_smoke.purpose: Prove the tracked offline luminance DXIL container + RTS0 root signature + descriptor layout can complete one minimal compute dispatch on a real D3D12 device/command queue.
device_requirements: real hardware D3D12 adapter (fake/null handles never accepted)
64-bit integer shader capability (Int64ShaderOps) on the device
signed DXC suite carrying dxil.dll so the compiler-emitted DXIL container can be signed in memory to load without Developer Mode; the tracked artifact bytes are never modified
MSVC vcvars64 to build the C++ D3D12 harness

status_semantics.success: root signature created directly from the Rurix RTS0 bytes, compute PSO created from the tracked DXIL container, SRV t0 / UAV u0 / b0 root constants bound from the descriptor layout, one minimal dispatch executed, fence completed, dst UAV read back; records adapter, artifact hashes, dispatch dimensions, fence value, and readback checksum
status_semantics.skip: no hardware D3D12 adapter / no D3D12 runtime / device lacks 64-bit integer capability / missing signed DXC suite / missing MSVC; records a concrete reason and does not advance readiness
status_semantics.fail: PSO / root signature / descriptor / resource / readback failure, artifact hash mismatch vs offline evidence, or descriptor-layout vs resource-mapping mismatch; keeps the D3D12 error code and log

segment_4c_real_d3d12_dispatch_smoke.on_missing_adapter_or_layout_mismatch: SKIP (no adapter) or FAIL (layout/hash mismatch); never returns success, never advances readiness
does_not_imply: real_d3d12_dispatch_recorded = true (the bridge still records no dispatch)
any visual diff, measured fallback telemetry, or performance claim

segment_4d_bridge_dispatch_recording_smo.purpose: Prove the Rurix Godot bridge (rurix_godot.dll built with the default-off d3d12-recording-shim feature) can record ONE minimal luminance compute dispatch on a real D3D12 device/command queue through its C ABI (rxgd_create_d3d12_session -> rxgd_register_texture -> rxgd_record_pass -> rxgd_collect_timestamps). Bridge smoke evidence only; not a Godot runtime pass.
segment_4d_bridge_dispatch_recording_smo.bridge_feature: d3d12-recording-shim (default OFF; not enabled in the shipping bridge or the Godot module build)
segment_4d_bridge_dispatch_recording_smo.recording_arm: harness-only capability flag RXGD_CAP_LUMINANCE_DISPATCH_RECORD (1u << 2) carried in RxGdCaps.flags; the Godot module never sets it, so the default runtime path never enters the recording arm
device_requirements: real hardware D3D12 adapter (fake/null handles never accepted)
64-bit integer shader capability (Int64ShaderOps) on the device
signed DXC suite carrying dxil.dll so the bridge shim can sign the in-memory DXIL container to load without Developer Mode; the tracked artifact bytes are never modified
MSVC vcvars64 to build the bridge DLL (cc-compiled shim) and the C++ harness
segment_4d_bridge_dispatch_recording_smo.ok_conditions: rxgd_record_pass may return RXGD_STATUS_OK for RXGD_PASS_LUMINANCE_REDUCTION only when the recording shim is linked, the harness passes real D3D12 device/queue and src/dst handles with the dispatch_bringup opt-in + record-arm flags + the 64-bit integer capability, and the embedded artifact bytes hash to the offline compile evidence digests. Otherwise the bridge falls back.

status_semantics.success: the bridge recorded one real dispatch: rxgd_record_pass returned RXGD_STATUS_OK, recorded_passes=1, fallback_passes=0, gpu_time_ns=0, the fence completed, and the dst UAV was read back; records adapter, artifact hashes, bridge dispatch dimensions, fence value, readback checksum, and bridge frame stats
status_semantics.skip: no hardware D3D12 adapter / no D3D12 runtime / device lacks 64-bit integer capability / missing signed DXC suite / missing MSVC; records a concrete reason and does not advance readiness
status_semantics.fail: DLL build/load failure, missing C ABI symbol, rxgd_record_pass not returning OK, inconsistent bridge frame stats, artifact hash mismatch vs offline evidence, or descriptor-layout vs resource-mapping mismatch

segment_4d_bridge_dispatch_recording_smo.gpu_timestamp_status: not_yet (no GPU timestamp implemented; gpu_time_ns is never fabricated and stays 0 / null)

evidence_artifact_hygiene.note: bridge_dispatch_recording_evidence.json is the artifact of ONE historical measured run, not a continuously recomputed live status.
evidence_artifact_hygiene.recorded_dll_fingerprint: the smoke records the feature-built DLL fingerprint (dll_fingerprint: dll_path_at_run, dll_sha256, dll_size_bytes, dll_mtime_utc, build_profile=debug, features=[d3d12-recording-shim]) plus an immutable snapshot copy under target/grx009_bridge_recording_smoke/rurix_godot_d3d12_recording_shim.dll (snapshot_dll_path/snapshot_dll_sha256)
evidence_artifact_hygiene.snapshot_binary_not_committed: the snapshot lives under target/ (gitignored) and is never committed to Git
evidence_artifact_hygiene.target_debug_dll_is_mutable: target/debug/rurix_godot.dll is a mutable build artifact; a later feature-off `cargo build -p rurix-godot` overwrites it in place, so its hash will no longer match the recorded feature-built DLL fingerprint
evidence_artifact_hygiene.readiness_does_not_gate_on_current_dll: 4d readiness stays true when the current target/debug/rurix_godot.dll no longer matches the recorded fingerprint, because the evidence is historical measured evidence; the probe emits a warning (not a failure) and exposes grx009_bridge_recording_evidence_dll_sha256, grx009_bridge_recording_current_dll_sha256, and grx009_bridge_recording_current_dll_matches_evidence
evidence_artifact_hygiene.refresh_command: $env:RURIX_DXC_DIR='H:\dxc-round7\extracted\bin\x64'; py -3 ci\grx009_luminance_bridge_recording_smoke.py (rerun to refresh the current artifact fingerprint and reproduce the exact feature-built DLL)

does_not_imply: any visual diff, measured fallback telemetry, GPU timestamp, or performance claim

segment_4e_native_resource_handle_mappin.purpose: Change the resource the Godot runtime hands to the Rurix bridge for the luminance pass from a logical Godot RID id into the real D3D12 ID3D12Resource* native handle, so a future Godot-runtime-driven recording path can bind real resources. This is native handle mapping preflight only; it is not a Godot runtime pass and does not enable the pass.
native_handle_mapping: source: rb->get_internal_texture() resolved to a real ID3D12Resource* via RenderingDevice::get_driver_resource
dest: luminance_buffers->reduce[0] resolved to a real ID3D12Resource* via RenderingDevice::get_driver_resource
either native handle resolving to 0 (or a missing RenderingDevice) forces the native Godot luminance fallback path
segment_4e_native_resource_handle_mappin.hook_parameter_change: D3D12Hooks / RurixAccelD3D12Hooks try_record_luminance_reduction parameters renamed from logical RID ids (p_source_texture_id / p_dest_texture_id) to real native handles (p_source_native_handle / p_dest_native_handle); RxGdResource.native_handle now carries the real ID3D12Resource*
segment_4e_native_resource_handle_mappin.shipping_bridge_behavior: the shipping/feature-off bridge still returns RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK for RXGD_PASS_LUMINANCE_REDUCTION; the Godot module never sets RXGD_CAP_LUMINANCE_DISPATCH_RECORD
does_not_imply: real_d3d12_dispatch_recorded = true (the default Godot runtime path records no dispatch)
any visual diff, measured fallback telemetry, GPU timestamp, or performance claim

segment_4f_godot_runtime_bridge_recordin.purpose: Prove the patched Godot runtime luminance call site (Auto Exposure) can drive ONE real bridge-recorded RXGD_PASS_LUMINANCE_REDUCTION D3D12 dispatch through the real ID3D12Resource* native handles it resolves in segment 4e, using a harness-only opt-in. This is test-only / harness-only Godot-runtime smoke evidence; it is not a default runtime pass.
segment_4f_godot_runtime_bridge_recordin.record_arm: harness-only capability flag RXGD_CAP_LUMINANCE_DISPATCH_RECORD (1u << 2) carried in RxGdCaps.flags; patch 0008 sets it ONLY when the default-false dispatch_recording_smoke opt-in is enabled, and it only has effect when the loaded rurix_godot.dll is built with the test-only d3d12-recording-shim feature
segment_4f_godot_runtime_bridge_recordin.requires_full_patch_stack: 0001..0008 (rebuilt Godot; the tracked external/godot-master build carries only 0001+0002+0003, so this smoke needs a scratch build pointed to by RURIX_GRX009_SEGMENT4F_GODOT_EXE)
segment_4f_godot_runtime_bridge_recordin.runtime_record_marker: RXGD_GODOT_RUNTIME_LUMINANCE_RECORD (printed by the module only when rxgd_record_pass returned RXGD_STATUS_OK for the Godot-runtime call site)
device_requirements: a Godot console exe rebuilt with the full 0001..0008 patch stack and module_rurix_accel_enabled=yes d3d12=yes (RURIX_GRX009_SEGMENT4F_GODOT_EXE)
real hardware D3D12 adapter (fake/null handles never accepted) with the 64-bit integer shader capability
signed DXC suite carrying dxil.dll so the recording shim can sign the in-memory DXIL container; the tracked artifact bytes are never modified
MSVC vcvars64 to build the d3d12-recording-shim rurix_godot.dll

status_semantics.success: the Godot runtime luminance call site drove one real bridge-recorded dispatch: the RXGD_GODOT_RUNTIME_LUMINANCE_RECORD marker reported recorded=1, artifact digests still match the offline evidence, and the descriptor layout matches the resource mapping; records adapter/exe/marker fields
status_semantics.skip: no segment-4f Godot scratch build / no real D3D12 adapter / no signed DXC suite / no MSVC / no bridge session / marker not observed; records a concrete reason and does not advance readiness
status_semantics.fail: artifact hash mismatch vs offline evidence, descriptor-layout vs resource-mapping mismatch, DLL build failure, or the marker present without recorded=1

does_not_imply: real_d3d12_dispatch_recorded = true (default Godot runtime path records no dispatch)
the shipping (feature-off) bridge or default Godot config returning RXGD_STATUS_OK
any visual diff, measured fallback telemetry, GPU timestamp, or performance claim

segment_4g_visual_fallback_gate.purpose: First REAL visual diff + measured fallback telemetry gate for the luminance_reduction path. Two deterministic runs of the tracked Godot build render the same minimal auto-exposure scene: the reference run keeps the per-pass setting at its default false, the candidate run enables it — the shipping (feature-off) bridge returns RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK (observed via the tracked patch-0002 marker), so the native Godot luminance path renders both frames and enabling the falling-back pass must not change the image. This validates the visual/fallback gate ONLY; it is NOT visual verification of a Rurix GPU pass (no such pass runs) and does not enable anything by default.
segment_4g_visual_fallback_gate.godot_build: tracked external/godot-master console build (patch stack 0001+0002+0003, module_rurix_accel_enabled=yes d3d12=yes), overridable via RURIX_GRX009_SEGMENT4G_GODOT_EXE; no scratch full-stack rebuild is required because the gate measures default/enabled-fallback behavior only
segment_4g_visual_fallback_gate.bridge_build: shipping feature-OFF rurix_godot.dll (no d3d12-recording-shim); the evidence dll_fingerprint must record an empty features list

pass_enable_matrix.disabled_default: reference run: rendering/rurix_accel/passes/luminance_reduction/enabled=false (default); the call site never invokes the bridge, so the fallback marker must NOT appear
pass_enable_matrix.enabled_fallback: candidate run: .../enabled=true; the Auto Exposure call site invokes the bridge, the bridge falls back, and the run MUST print the tracked patch-0002 marker 'RurixAccel: luminance_reduction fallback rc=' (the measured fallback-path-observed signal)

visual_metric.anti_fabrication: the probe re-verifies the frame artifact SHA-256 digests, requires file size == width*height*3, recomputes the LDR diff (including the diff artifact bytes) in-process, and requires the recorded numbers and pinned thresholds to match; a hand-edited or placeholder success JSON can never advance the gate

status_semantics.success: both runs exit 0 with a ready bridge session, the candidate run observed the fallback marker (and the reference run did not), real RGB8 frames were captured with matching dimensions, the LDR absolute diff sits within the pinned thresholds, and the generated measured_local GRX-008 telemetry document passes fallback_telemetry.py --validate-only; success publishes the tracked frame artifacts + telemetry and writes the historical success evidence
status_semantics.skip: missing tracked Godot exe / run timeout / no ready bridge session / fallback marker not observed in this environment; records a concrete reason and does not advance readiness (RURIX_REQUIRE_REAL=1 turns SKIP into FAIL)
status_semantics.fail: artifact hash mismatch vs offline evidence, DLL build failure, non-zero Godot exit, capture failure, dimension mismatch, LDR diff over threshold, fallback marker in the disabled run, or an invalid generated telemetry document

segment_4g_visual_fallback_gate.evidence_artifact_hygiene: two-file latest/success pattern mirroring segment 4f: visual_fallback_evidence.json is rewritten on every run (reproducible-default SKIP without the tracked exe) and never advances the gate on its own; visual_fallback_success_evidence.json is written only on a strict status=success run and is never deleted or overwritten by a later SKIP/FAIL run, so the readiness gate advances off the historical measured success only
does_not_imply: Rurix-pass visual verification (both frames come from the native Godot luminance path; no Rurix GPU pass runs)
any GPU timestamp, FPS, or performance improvement claim

segment_4h_real_pass_enablement_gate.purpose: Gated REAL luminance pass enablement gate: the first explicit opt-in arm (rendering/rurix_accel/passes/luminance_reduction/dispatch_real_pass, default false, patch 0009) for an actual Rurix real pass. The bridge real-pass attempt runs runtime binding preflight, dispatch eligibility, the per-slot kernel-binding-kind conformance check, and the math-parity gate; with the stage A2-A5 texture-capable hlsl_bridge canonical package every software gate can pass, and under a d3d12-recording-shim bridge DLL the linked real dispatch may return RXGD_STATUS_OK. When the dispatch cannot complete the gate honestly reports first_missing_prerequisite=real_dispatch_recording_failed (feature-off shipping bridge: real_dispatch_path_not_linked) instead of claiming a real GPU pass. This is a bring-up gate, NOT a default enablement.
segment_4h_real_pass_enablement_gate.godot_build: scratch Godot console build carrying the FULL 0001..0009 patch stack (module_rurix_accel_enabled=yes d3d12=yes), provided via RURIX_GRX009_SEGMENT4H_GODOT_EXE plus the RURIX_GRX009_SEGMENT4H_GODOT_SOURCE / _SOURCE_PROVENANCE / _BUILD_COMMAND / _BUILD_LOG env vars; the tracked external/godot-master build only has 0001+0002+0003 and must NOT be reused
segment_4h_real_pass_enablement_gate.bridge_build: shipping feature-OFF rurix_godot.dll: the segment 4h real-pass gate chain is compiled into the shipping bridge and is fail-closed there, so the gate measures the exact DLL that ships (the evidence dll_fingerprint must record an empty features list)

pass_enable_matrix.disabled_default: reference run: all per-pass settings at their false defaults; no bridge invocation, no markers
pass_enable_matrix.enabled_real_pass_optin: candidate run: .../enabled=true + .../dispatch_bringup=true + .../dispatch_real_pass=true; with the stage A5 d3d12-recording-shim bridge the real-pass attempt may complete a real dispatch and print the 'RXGD_GODOT_RUNTIME_LUMINANCE_REAL_PASS' marker; otherwise the run MUST print both the tracked 0007-level fallback marker and the once-per-session 'RXGD_REAL_PASS_BLOCKED first_missing_prerequisite=real_dispatch_recording_failed' diagnostic
pass_enable_matrix.forced_capability_downgrade: forced-failure red leg: candidate settings plus .../real_pass_force_capability_downgrade=true (harness-only knob; the module clears the shader-int64 capability), so the bridge preflight fails closed with unsupported_device while the native Godot path keeps rendering

visual_metric.scope: measured_local real frames: the candidate and forced-failure frames must match the native reference frame within the pinned LDR thresholds (arming the fail-closed opt-in must not change the image); on a future strict success the same thresholds gate the native reference vs the real Rurix-pass candidate frame, and an over-threshold diff is a FAIL that keeps the pass default disabled

outcome_semantics.skip_environment: a precondition (scratch exe, auditable provenance sidecar, ready session) is unavailable; RURIX_REQUIRE_REAL=1 upgrades this to FAIL
outcome_semantics.skip_measured_prerequisite_blocked: every leg ran and measured EXACTLY the predicted fail-closed shape; the evidence records first_missing_prerequisite (today: kernel_binding_kind_mismatch, so a runtime-mappable texture-capable math-parity kernel artifact round is required) and never advances the gate
outcome_semantics.fail: any integrity violation (unexpected markers, over-threshold diff, invalid telemetry, unexpected ERROR lines, non-zero exits, tampered artifacts)
outcome_semantics.success: strict and currently unreachable by design: the opt-in real dispatch executed AND completed (RXGD_GODOT_RUNTIME_LUMINANCE_REAL_PASS observed) AND the visual gate stayed green AND every audit passed; only then is real_gpu_pass=true recorded in the historical success artifact, and even then default_enable_state stays disabled and performance_claim stays none

compile_blockers.summary: the canonical texture-capable package is the owner-approved hlsl_bridge_workaround artifact (DXC cs_6_0 container validated by dxv, per-slot texture2d/rwtexture2d binding kinds, Rurix-owned RTS0); a rurixc-owned texture-capable compile still requires a patched llc that supports llvm.dx.resource.load.texture.2d

implementation_status.runtime_state_note: The DEFAULT runtime path stays fallback-only: with all per-pass settings at their false defaults the bridge still returns RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK for RXGD_PASS_LUMINANCE_REDUCTION and the native Godot luminance path renders. real_gpu_pass=true records the segment 4h strict MEASURED success of the opt-in arm (dispatch_real_pass under a d3d12-recording-shim bridge on the 0001..0010 scratch build); it is not a default enablement and carries no performance claim.
implementation_status.bridge_gate: src/rurix-godot LuminanceReductionGate: default disabled; request_enable and request_dispatch_bringup both remain fallback_only for GRX-009 segment 4b; rxgd_record_pass runs record_gated_dispatch_bringup for RXGD_PASS_LUMINANCE_REDUCTION (segment 4a runtime binding preflight, then a dispatch eligibility gate: RXGD_CAP_LUMINANCE_DISPATCH_BRINGUP opt-in flag, 64-bit integer shader capability, non-null native D3D12 device/queue and resource handles, and a compiled package whose descriptor layout and offline evidence digests match) but still returns RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK even when every precondition passes, records no D3D12 dispatch, and accumulates no estimated GPU/CPU time
implementation_status.godot_module_patch: spike/godot-rurix/patches/0002-rurix-accel-luminance-pass-gate.patch (module-scoped; per-pass setting rendering/rurix_accel/passes/luminance_reduction/enabled defaults to false; try_record_luminance_reduction() returns false on any non-OK bridge status)
implementation_status.godot_core_callsite_patch: spike/godot-rurix/patches/0003-rurix-accel-luminance-core-callsite-wiring.patch (stacked on 0001+0002; adds a default-false virtual D3D12Hooks::try_record_luminance_reduction(); wires servers/rendering/renderer_rd/renderer_scene_render_rd.cpp Auto Exposure to call the opt-in gate before the native luminance_reduction and skip it only when the gate returns true)

wired_call_sites.note: opt-in gate calls D3D12Hooks::try_record_luminance_reduction() before native luminance_reduction

implementation_status.segment_3a_last_result_note: canonical offline_compile_evidence.json records status=success with runtime_mappable=true under the owner-approved hlsl_bridge_workaround provenance: the canonical artifacts/ paths (luminance_reduction.dxil, .rts0.bin, _descriptor_layout.json) carry the texture-capable hlsl_bridge package (DXC cs_6_0 DXIL container validated by dxv, Rurix-owned RTS0 root signature from rurixc::binding_layout::serialize_rts0, per-slot texture2d/rwtexture2d binding kinds). The bridge tracked package is texture-capable (LUMINANCE_KERNEL_RESOURCE_BINDING_KINDS=[texture2d, rwtexture2d]). The historical raw-buffer segment 3a fixture is retained in offline_compile_evidence_raw_buffer.json / artifacts/raw_buffer_historical/. A rurixc-owned texture-capable compile still requires a patched llc that supports llvm.dx.resource.load.texture.2d. Default runtime remains fallback_only (real dispatch only under the d3d12-recording-shim feature plus the opt-in real-pass arm) and no measured real GPU pass success exists yet
implementation_status.segment_3b_patch: spike/godot-rurix/patches/0004-rurix-accel-luminance-resource-mapping-scaffold.patch (stacked scaffold; module/bridge mapping markers only; does not make the bridge return OK)
implementation_status.segment_3b_last_result_note: resource mapping scaffold records Godot source/reduce/current luminance resources, source dimensions, min/max/exposure parameters, descriptor bindings b0/t0/u0, and the required 64-bit integer shader capability gate. Runtime remains fallback_only and no real GPU pass exists
implementation_status.segment_4a_patch: spike/godot-rurix/patches/0005-rurix-accel-luminance-runtime-binding-preflight.patch (stacked on 0001+0002+0003+0004; extends the D3D12Hooks luminance hook so the Godot side passes the real luminance binding: logical RID ids, source/destination dimensions, and the b0 root constant scalars; the bridge still returns RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK and records no D3D12 dispatch)
implementation_status.segment_4a_last_result_note: runtime binding preflight wires the segment 3b resource mapping scaffold into a bridge-side validation layer: rxgd_record_pass validates the b0/t0/u0 descriptor shape, the 28-byte push constant block, the source dimensions against the bound src_luminance resource, the max(source / 8, 1) level-0 reduce shape of dst_luminance, and the 64-bit integer shader capability. Validation failure returns fallback with a recorded reason and no estimated GPU/CPU time; validation success still returns RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK because no real dispatch path exists. Runtime remains fallback_only, real_gpu_pass stays false, no D3D12 dispatch is recorded, and no visual diff, measured telemetry, or performance claim is made
implementation_status.segment_4b_patch: spike/godot-rurix/patches/0006-rurix-accel-luminance-gated-dispatch-bringup.patch (stacked on 0001+0002+0003+0004+0005; adds a default-false rendering/rurix_accel/passes/luminance_reduction/dispatch_bringup opt-in, declares the reserved RXGD_CAP_LUMINANCE_DISPATCH_BRINGUP flag carried in RxGdCaps.flags without any ABI struct layout change, and sets that flag in try_create_session only when the opt-in setting is enabled; the bridge still returns RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK and records no D3D12 dispatch)
implementation_status.segment_4b_last_result_note: gated dispatch bring-up puts an explicit, opt-in dispatch eligibility gate in front of the segment 4a runtime binding preflight. rxgd_record_pass runs record_gated_dispatch_bringup: it re-runs the full preflight, then checks the Godot opt-in flag (RXGD_CAP_LUMINANCE_DISPATCH_BRINGUP), the 64-bit integer shader capability, non-null native D3D12 device/queue handles, non-null resource native handles, and that the compiled package descriptor layout and offline compile evidence digests (DXIL, root signature, descriptor layout SHA-256) match. Any failure returns RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK with a recorded reason. Even when every precondition passes, request_dispatch_bringup keeps the explicit dispatch gate closed: segment 4c has a standalone measured D3D12 dispatch smoke, but there is no bridge-linked runtime dispatch recording path, no measured bridge telemetry, and rxgd_record_pass must not return OK, so the pass still returns RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK, records no D3D12 dispatch, and accumulates no estimated GPU/CPU time. The standalone segment 4c real D3D12 dispatch smoke (ci/grx009_luminance_d3d12_dispatch_smoke.py) has since completed with status=success on a real D3D12 adapter (see segment_4c_real_d3d12_dispatch_smoke), so the next action is start_grx009_bridge_real_d3d12_dispatch_recording. That smoke is standalone measured evidence only: the bridge still records no dispatch, runtime remains fallback_only, real_gpu_pass stays false, real_d3d12_dispatch_recorded stays false, and no visual diff, measured telemetry, or performance claim is made
implementation_status.real_local_d3d12_dispatch_smoke: segment 4c: ci/grx009_luminance_d3d12_dispatch_smoke.py records measured status=success in spike/godot-rurix/passes/luminance_reduction/real_d3d12_dispatch_smoke.json (real D3D12 device/queue, Rurix RTS0 accepted, compute PSO from the tracked DXIL, minimal dispatch + fence completion + dst UAV readback). disclaimer: GRX-009 segment 4b gated dispatch bring-up adds an explicit, opt-in dispatch eligibility gate in front of the segment 4a runtime binding preflight. Patch 0006 adds a default-false rendering/rurix_accel/passes/luminance_reduction/dispatch_bringup opt-in, declares the reserved RXGD_CAP_LUMINANCE_DISPATCH_BRINGUP flag carried in RxGdCaps.flags (no ABI struct layout change, RXGD_ABI_VERSION stays 1), and sets that flag only when the opt-in is enabled. The bridge checks the opt-in flag, the 64-bit integer capability, non-null native D3D12 device/queue and resource handles, and that the compiled package layout/digests match the offline evidence; even when every precondition passes the explicit dispatch gate stays closed, so rxgd_record_pass still returns RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK for RXGD_PASS_LUMINANCE_REDUCTION, records no D3D12 dispatch, and accumulates no estimated GPU/CPU time. The per-pass settings default to disabled and the native Godot luminance path takes over in practice. GRX-009 segment 4c adds a real Windows D3D12 dispatch smoke (ci/grx009_luminance_d3d12_dispatch_smoke.py) whose measured evidence (real_d3d12_dispatch_smoke.json) records status=success: the tracked luminance DXIL container + RTS0 root signature + descriptor layout completed one minimal compute dispatch on a real D3D12 device/command queue (root signature accept, compute PSO from the tracked DXIL, minimal dispatch, fence completion, dst UAV readback). That smoke is standalone measured evidence only; it does not make the bridge record a dispatch or return OK, does not enable the Godot luminance Rurix path, and keeps runtime_state=fallback_only, real_gpu_pass=false, and real_d3d12_dispatch_recorded=false. GRX-009 segment 4d adds a bridge real D3D12 dispatch recording smoke (ci/grx009_luminance_bridge_recording_smoke.py) whose measured evidence (bridge_dispatch_recording_evidence.json) records status=success: rurix_godot.dll built with the default-off d3d12-recording-shim feature recorded ONE real luminance compute dispatch on a real D3D12 device/queue through its C ABI (rxgd_record_pass returned RXGD_STATUS_OK, recorded_passes=1, fallback_passes=0, fence completed, dst UAV read back). The recording path is compiled only under the test-only feature and armed only by the harness-only RXGD_CAP_LUMINANCE_DISPATCH_RECORD flag; the Godot module never sets it and the shipping (feature-off) bridge is unchanged and still returns RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK for RXGD_PASS_LUMINANCE_REDUCTION. That bridge smoke is measured evidence only; it does not enable the Godot luminance Rurix path (godot_runtime_luminance_path_enabled=false), does not complete a Godot runtime pass, and keeps runtime_state=fallback_only, real_gpu_pass=false, real_d3d12_dispatch_recorded=false, default_enable_state=disabled, and gpu_timestamp_status=not_yet. GRX-009 segment 4e adds native D3D12 resource handle mapping (patch 0007): the Godot runtime now resolves the real D3D12 ID3D12Resource* native handles for rb->get_internal_texture() and luminance_buffers->reduce[0] through RenderingDevice::get_driver_resource(DRIVER_RESOURCE_TEXTURE, RID, 0) and hands those real handles (not logical RID ids) to the bridge in RxGdResource.native_handle, falling back to the native Godot luminance path when RenderingDevice is unavailable or either native handle resolves to 0. This is native handle mapping preflight only: RXGD_ABI_VERSION is unchanged, the pass stays default-disabled, the Godot module never sets RXGD_CAP_LUMINANCE_DISPATCH_RECORD, and the shipping/feature-off bridge still returns RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK for RXGD_PASS_LUMINANCE_REDUCTION. It does not enable the Godot luminance Rurix path, does not complete a Godot runtime pass, and keeps runtime_state=fallback_only, real_gpu_pass=false, and real_d3d12_dispatch_recorded=false. GRX-009 segment 4f adds a Godot-runtime bridge dispatch recording smoke (patch 0008 + ci/grx009_godot_runtime_bridge_recording_smoke.py): patch 0008 adds a default-false rendering/rurix_accel/passes/luminance_reduction/dispatch_recording_smoke opt-in and declares the harness-only RXGD_CAP_LUMINANCE_DISPATCH_RECORD flag in the module, which try_create_session sets ONLY when that opt-in is enabled (RXGD_ABI_VERSION stays 1, no struct layout change), and try_record_luminance_reduction prints the RXGD_GODOT_RUNTIME_LUMINANCE_RECORD marker only when rxgd_record_pass returned RXGD_STATUS_OK. This lets the patched Godot runtime luminance call site drive one real bridge-recorded dispatch through the segment 4e native handles, but only under the test-only opt-in plus a d3d12-recording-shim rurix_godot.dll and only on a Godot scratch build rebuilt with the full 0001..0008 stack (RURIX_GRX009_SEGMENT4F_GODOT_EXE); an unavailable scratch build, real D3D12 adapter, signed DXC, or MSVC records a concrete SKIP that does not advance readiness. By default (no opt-in enabled) the Godot module still never sets RXGD_CAP_LUMINANCE_DISPATCH_RECORD, and the shipping/feature-off bridge and default Godot config still return RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK for RXGD_PASS_LUMINANCE_REDUCTION. Even a segment 4f success keeps runtime_state=fallback_only, real_gpu_pass=false, real_d3d12_dispatch_recorded=false, godot_runtime_luminance_path_enabled=false, default_enable_state=disabled, and gpu_timestamp_status=not_yet. GRX-009 segment 4g adds the first real visual diff + measured fallback telemetry gate (ci/grx009_segment4g_visual_fallback_smoke.py): two deterministic runs of the tracked Godot build (patch stack 0001+0002+0003) render the same minimal auto-exposure scene with the per-pass setting default-false (reference) vs true (candidate); the candidate run's Auto Exposure call site invokes the shipping feature-off bridge, which returns RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK (measured via the tracked patch-0002 'RurixAccel: luminance_reduction fallback rc=' marker), so the native Godot luminance path renders BOTH frames, and the captured raw RGB8 frames must match within the pinned LDR absolute-diff thresholds (max_abs<=2, mean_abs<=0.25). The measured success evidence (visual_fallback_success_evidence.json, hash-pinned frame artifacts under artifacts/visual/, and the measured_local GRX-008 measured_fallback_telemetry.json) proves ONLY that the fallback path is really taken and preserves visuals: it is NOT visual verification of a Rurix GPU pass (no Rurix pass runs in either frame), it does not enable the pass by default, and it keeps runtime_state=fallback_only, real_gpu_pass=false, real_d3d12_dispatch_recorded=false, godot_runtime_luminance_path_enabled=false, and default_enable_state=disabled. No real GPU pass, no default Godot runtime bridge-recorded D3D12 dispatch, no GPU timestamp, no Rurix-pass visual verification, and no performance or FPS improvement is claimed; the only visual/telemetry evidence held is the measured fallback-path gate above.
